title: Dynamically replicate/sync/copy all additional tags of a specific imagename:tagname - is that possible?
body:
Hi!

My problem is somewhat special... I need to copy images between registries, but have to preserve all of their individual tags (which are dynamically changing over time).

I.e. if I do

`skopeo copy docker://A.priv.reg/repo/imagename:latest docker://B.priv.reg/repo/imagename:latest`

and `repo/imagename` has additional tags, like `:v1_step1_pre`, `:v1_step1_cust` while executing `skopeo copy` for the first time and `:master_post`, `:master_final` when executing `skopeo copy` on another day a second time, how can I force skopeo to

a) copy the image and all of the additional tags (`:v1_step1_pre`, `:v1_step1_cust`) on the first run automatically
b) _change_ the tags (only!) on the next run, if they changed like in the example above(remove: `:v1_step1_pre`, `:v1_step1_cust`, add: `:master_post`, `:master_final`)?

(Tags can contain any kind of information and their structure can change. The examples above are only to make it more clear what I am trying to explain and do not represent a fixed structure or layout.)

I know, it sounds weird, but...

Regards,
Holger

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

Does this _need_ to be limited to only the same tags of the `:latest` image, or is it basically all of the tags in the repo? `skopeo sync` can copy an arbitrary set of tags (and with `--preserve-digests`, should preserve the tag relationships, i.e. two tags pointing at the same image, _assuming that doesn’t change during the copy). If the tags keep pointing at the same image, there is _some_ overhead but the full binaries should not be copied again.

Or do you mean something much more specific?
Thanks for writing back so quickly :-) No, it has to be limited to only the tags of the `:latest` image.

That doesn’t exist as a native feature, primarily because “see all tags with the same digest” is not a native feature of the registry API and it’s rather expensive.

I think a script doing that could be built using a combination of `skopeo list-tags` + `skopeo inspect` (to determine the relevant tags) + `skopeo copy` (to do the tag propagation).

Yes, I know that the API does not provide the necessary information and one has to solve it with different API requests and combining all the data. You're right, it should be possible to build a script. I thought about building one but did not want to reinvent the wheel if skopeo had been capable of this already :-)

Thanks you.
